meaning: n.制圆,舍入,凑整adj.圆的,环绕的,凑整的
examples: He had sought work as a cowboy, rounding up cattle.
They're going house to house, rounding up the residents.
In the distance, people could see the train rounding a bend, headed into the station.
Rounding out the motion controls is strafing, which can be done by tilting the controller left and right.
For astute handicappers, that was a sign that the colt was rounding into top form.
